summary: AS080 is a COMPACT CADASTRAL SURVEY -- a 6-STEP SURVEYOR'S ROUTE with distances and landmarks held at the Musee de l'Homme, Paris. 40 cords. All 4 STRING words are CANONICAL CADASTRAL MARKERS at 100 PERCENT DICT HIT: paqa (east orientation), chiqa (benchmark / verification), taqa (boundary), kaqa (wall). Large initial measurement 170 units suggests long distances (possibly stadia or tupu units). Cluster 1 carries a 6-WAYPOINT ROUTE: position 1 sv=170 + paqa east, position 2 sv=70 with sub-measures 60 + 10, position 3 taqa boundary with measures 20 + 30, position 4 sv=50, position 5 kaqa natural wall + sv=10, position 6 sv=10 arriving at chiqa benchmark. Cluster 2 = detail register (8 cords, multi-colour L2). Cluster 3 = closure (2 cords). Colours: BS (brown-sand) = main measurements, GL (olive-green) = annotations / terrain, W = data, R = special markers. SAME FORMAT AS HP020 (Pachacamac treasure-route card): route + landmarks + distances. AS080 is the cleanest corpus exemplar of the COMPACT ROUTE-CADASTRE sub-type.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  40 cords. Musee de l'Homme, Paris. Provenance unknown.
  4 STRING words at 100 percent dict hit, all CADASTRAL:
    paqa (east), chiqa (benchmark / verification), taqa (boundary), kaqa (wall).

FORMAT -- 6-WAYPOINT ROUTE
  Cluster 1: 6-position route with distances and landmarks.
    Pos 1: sv=170 units + paqa = 170 units EAST
    Pos 2: sv=70 units, sub-measures 60 + 10
    Pos 3: taqa (BOUNDARY) with measures 20 + 30
    Pos 4: sv=50 units
    Pos 5: kaqa (WALL = natural limit) + sv=10 units
    Pos 6: sv=10 units, arrives at chiqa (BENCHMARK)
  Cluster 2: detail register (8 cords, multi-colour L2 subsidiaries).
  Cluster 3: closure (2 cords).

COLOURS
  BS (brown-sand)   = MAIN MEASUREMENTS
  GL (olive-green)  = ANNOTATIONS / terrain description
  W  (white)        = DATA reference channel
  R  (red)          = SPECIAL MARKERS

SAME FORMAT AS HP020
  HP020 (Pachacamac treasure-route card, 29 cords) also uses the compact route + landmarks + distances
  convention. AS080 + HP020 = two instances of the COMPACT ROUTE-CADASTRE sub-type.
narrative:
One hundred and seventy units east. That is where AS080 begins. The first cord carries the distance, the second carries the word paqa -- the cardinal direction.

Seventy units next. Two sub-measures: sixty plus ten. The route bends, crosses a fold in the terrain, resumes.

At position three, a BOUNDARY. Taqa. With sub-measures twenty and thirty. The boundary has thickness -- the surveyor notes not just that the line exists but how wide the zone of demarcation is.

Fifty units more. Then a WALL. Kaqa -- natural limit, an outcrop or cliff or constructed barrier. Ten units up against it.

Another ten units. And then, at the sixth position, the BENCHMARK. Chiqa. The verification point. The terminal landmark of the route. The surveyor has arrived.

A detail register follows in cluster two -- eight cords carrying subsidiary annotations about the terrain traversed. Two closing cords end the document in cluster three.

Twenty-three distance units in the route, summed across six waypoints (one hundred and seventy, seventy, fifty, ten, ten, plus the boundary and wall sub-measures) -- several hundred tupu, several kilometres at least. A linear sweep across a specific precinct, from an eastward origin to a final benchmark. A survey of exactly one line.

At the Musee de l'Homme, this forty-cord route sits alongside AS076 (the naming ceremony), AS077 (the zone inventory), and AS075 (the Pachacamac pilgrimage register) -- four compact Inca documents of four different functions in one Paris collection. AS080 is the cleanest of them, the one whose purpose is unmistakable: a surveyor's path, knotted into cotton, still readable five hundred years after its survey.

prior_work.claim: Inca surveying used standardised distance units (tupu ~1.6 km, stadia, pace counts) and canonical cadastral vocabulary (paqa, taqa, kaqa, chiqa, qaqa, waqa); route-surveys walked linear paths between named landmarks.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu; Hyslop, J. (1984). The Inka Road System.

prior_work.claim: The Musee de l'Homme in Paris holds AS080 alongside AS075, AS076, AS077 -- a 4-document compact-khipu cluster acquired during the 19th-20th century European collecting era.
HP020 (Pachacamac treasure-route card, Museo de Sitio de Pachacamac) uses the same compact route + landmark + distance format as AS080 -- the convention is cross-repository attested.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Pachacamac treasure dossier analysis; docs/ALBA_BRIEF_HP019_HP020_PACHACAMAC_TRESOR.md.

v3_contribution.claim: CLEANEST COMPACT ROUTE-CADASTRE EXEMPLAR: 4/4 canonical cadastral markers (paqa, taqa, kaqa, chiqa) at 100 percent dict hit -- among the highest signature-purity ratings in the cadastral sub-corpus. All 4 words present and all correctly glossed.

evidence.test: cadastral-marker purity + dict-hit ratio across cadastral sub-corpus

v3_contribution.claim: 6-WAYPOINT LINEAR ROUTE: cluster 1 encodes a surveyor's path with position-by-position distance + landmark annotations. sv=170 + paqa (east), sv=70 + sub-measures, taqa, sv=50, kaqa + sv=10, sv=10 + chiqa. Linear route structure is observable in the cord cadence.

ROUTE-CADASTRE SUB-TYPE matches HP020 (Pachacamac treasure card): same 6-step-route + landmarks + distances convention. AS080 + HP020 = two cross-museum instances of the route-cadastre sub-type, distinct from the full-survey sub-type (UR1095, UR016).

evidence.test: AS080 vs HP020 route-cadastre structural format match

v3_contribution.claim: COLOUR-ROLE SPECIALISATION: BS (brown-sand) = measurements, GL (olive-green) = annotations, R (red) = special markers. Multi-colour palette matches the route's mixed content (distances + terrain notes + exceptions).

companions.note: STRUCTURAL TWIN -- Pachacamac treasure-route card (29 cords). Same compact-route-cadastre format. AS080 is the Paris-museum version; HP020 is the Pachacamac-museum version. Two instances of the same convention across two collections.
Full Pachacamac cadastral survey (212 cords, siqa x5). AS080 is the compact-route summary; UR1095 is the full-scale site plan.
Leymebamba 7-sector cadastral survey (442 cords). AS080 traces one linear route; UR016 maps an entire 7-sector district.
Arica cadastral register. Cross-site comparison for the cadastral-vocabulary signature.
Incahuasi UE 14 sparse event register with compressed paqa-taqa-kaqa anchoring formula. AS080's linear route uses paqa -> taqa -> kaqa -> chiqa in the same cadastral sequence at different scale.
Musee de l'Homme Pachacamac pilgrimage register. Paris-museum co-catalogued.
Musee de l'Homme rutuchikuy naming ceremony. Paris-museum co-catalogued.
Musee de l'Homme zone inventory. Paris-museum co-catalogued; AS075/AS076/AS077/AS080 form the 4-document Paris set.

word_reading.note: 4/4 canonical cadastral markers at 100 percent dict hit. paqa, chiqa, taqa, kaqa all dictionary-stable, cross-verified against UR273A + UR275 + UR1087 cadastral conventions.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey route-cadastre sub-type robustly supported: full 4/4 cadastral signature + 6-waypoint linear route cadence + cross-museum HP020 structural parallel. Clean classification, no competing signature fires.

narrative.note: The '6-waypoint surveyor's route with distances + boundary + wall + benchmark' reading is internally coherent and directly anchored on the cord-by-cord cluster-1 structural observations. The specific tupu / stadia unit interpretation is plausible but not decisive; the relative distance ratios are preserved regardless.

data_integrity.note: 100 percent dict hit. Small 40-cord document limits arithmetic cross-checks but the cadastral signature is unambiguous. No Ascher layer summarised. Provenance unknown limits external anchoring to HP020 parallel.

sections.title: 6-Waypoint Surveyor's Route
sections.reading: The document's core. Six route positions with distances and landmarks. Pos 1 sv=170 + paqa (170 units east). Pos 2 sv=70 + sub-measures 60 + 10. Pos 3 taqa + sub-measures 20 + 30 (boundary zone). Pos 4 sv=50. Pos 5 kaqa + sv=10 (wall limit). Pos 6 sv=10 + chiqa (benchmark arrival).

sections.title: Detail Register
sections.reading: Subsidiary annotations about the terrain traversed. Multi-colour L2 cords carry per-waypoint detail not in the main route.

sections.title: Closure
sections.reading: Two closing cords. Likely total distance or route-identifier seal.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Hyslop, J. (1984). The Inka Road System.
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- paqa, taqa, chiqa base glosses.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).
Musee de l'Homme, Paris -- AS080 catalogue record.
Museo de Sitio de Pachacamac -- HP020 structural parallel.
Compare HP020 (Pachacamac treasure-route card), UR1095 (full Pachacamac cadastre), UR016 (Leymebamba 7-sector), UR273A (compressed cadastral formula), AS075/AS076/AS077 (Paris-museum set).
